title: Modules 2
description: The `use` keyword brings paths into scope so you don't have to write the full path each time.
instructions: Add the correct `use` statement to bring the function into scope.
hints: The `pub use` statement re-exports items, making them accessible through the parent module.
This code should compile as-is because `pub use` creates public aliases.
The `use` keyword with `self::` refers to items in the current module.
explanation: The `use` keyword creates shortcuts to items in modules. `pub use` re-exports items, making them available to external code through a different path. The `as` keyword creates an alias. `self::` refers to the current module. This pattern is commonly used to create a clean public API from internally organized code.
